lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Greetings, desert traveller. Do you require the services of Ali Morrisane's flying carpet fleet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: From here you can travel to Uzer, to the Bedabin camp or to the North of Pollnivneach.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: The second major carpet hub station, to the south of Pollnivneach is in easy walking distance from there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to travel to Uzer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to travel to the Bedabin Camp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to travel to Pollnivneach.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't want to travel to any of those places.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Fair enough, magic carpet travel isn't for everyone. Enjoy the walk.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: You can travel from here back to the Shantay Pass.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to travel to Uzer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't want to travel there.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Fair enough, magic carpet travel isn't for everyone. Enjoy the walk.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: From here you can travel to the Shantay Pass.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take me there.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: From here you can travel to the Shantay Pass - the Southern gate of Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Take me to the Pass then.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: From here you can travel to Nardah and the Menaphite cities of Sophanem and Menaphos.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to travel to Nardah.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to travel to Menaphos.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to travel to Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: The carpets here will take you to the south of Pollnivneach.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go then.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: The carpets here will take you to the south of Pollnivneach. Do you want to take a lift?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pollnivneach will do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Ali Morrisane.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: What, you haven't heard of Ali M? Possibly the greatest salesman of the Kharidian empire if not all Gielinor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't say that I have, but he must be the ambitious type to try and set up his own airline.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: You know something, I reckon that he's trying to take on those gnomes at their own game and I'd bet good money that he'll probably win.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hah? I think you've gone and lost me now.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: You know those small little guys, not the dwarves now mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ya... gnomes, I'm with you that far.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Well they have already established an Airline, Gnome Air...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go on...

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Anyway I think that Ali M's setup here will prove really successful and maybe once we're properly established we could try compete with those gnomes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll watch this space.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes I remember him now, I went on a wild goose chase looking for his nephew.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Ha! No doubt old Ali M instigated the whole thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had a bit of fun though, The whole job was quite diverting.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: There's never a dull moment around that man, he's always looking for a way to make a quick coin or two.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about this Magic Carpet fleet.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: The latest idea from the great Ali Morrisane. Desert travel will never be the same again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how does it work?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: The carpet or the whole enterprise?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about how the carpet works.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: I'm not really too sure, it's just an enchanted rug really, made out of special Ugthanki hair. It flies to whatever destination its owner commands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are they for sale then?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Do you think I'm mad? Do you think that Ali Morrisane would throw his magic carpet monopoly away?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well perhaps if I offered the right price?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Not a hope. Could you imagine the mess there'd be if people were constantly zooming through Al Kharid and Pollnivneach? It would be chaos. This way, we can keep the carpet traffic outside towns and other busy places.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose getting stuck in a carpet jam could get a bit tiresome.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Just think of the friction burns you would get if you were in a carpet crash.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the enterprise then.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: It's quite simple really, Ali Morrisane has hired myself and a few others to set up carpet stations at some of the desert's more populated places and run flights between the stations.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So why has he limited the service to just the desert?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: I don't think Ali is prepared to take on Gnome Air just yet, their gliders are much faster than our carpets, besides that I think we are in the short haul business, something that would only work in harsh conditions like

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: the desert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is that?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: I suppose because people would just walk. Getting lost isn't too much of a problem generally, but it's a different matter when you're in the middle of the Kharidian desert with a dry waterskin and no idea

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: which direction to go in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're right I guess. How's the business going then?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Not too bad, the hubs are generally quite busy. But the stations in Uzer and the Bedabin camp could do with a bit more traffic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A growth market I guess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some questions.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: I'll try to help you as much as I can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Well this is a good position for desert traffic. Shantay seems to have a nice little money spinner setup, but I reckon, this could turn out even better.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: You mightn't realise it, but this is quite a busy station.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who would want to come here?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Well you for one, and besides that we get quite a few archaeologists from the dig site passing through to examine the golem.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Well besides the obvious - looking after this station, I'm trying to figure out how these Tentis manage to cultivate such delicious pineapples.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Well Pollnivneach is the ideal location for setting up a carpet station.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why's that?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: You see it's located halfway between Al Kharid, and the Menaphite cities and close enough to Nardah too, so we get more than enough traffic to keep the business running.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: I work here renting out magic carpets. I'm from Pollnivneach so it is a handy job, I don't have to commute too far to work every day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why's that?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: You see it's located halfway between Al Kharid, and the Menaphite cities and close enough to Nardah too, so we get more than enough traffic to keep the business running.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I suppose you're called Ali then.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Not the most remarkable of names, not that it matters, you see everyone in town knows me as Flash.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh right.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Well I'd preferred to have been running one of the carpet stations at a hub such as Pollnivneach. I was a bit slow off the mark to get that gig though. Still business in Nardah isn't bad for a terminal. At least

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: people come here for the bank and to see the herbalist.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: I look after the carpet station here. The place is a bit dead though. Ha! I'm just too much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: You know, Sophanem, city of the dead and all that?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Aw come on, the joke wasn't that bad.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Until recently I was looking after one of the busiest carpet stations. But that all changed since Menaphos closed its gates.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Right now I have to fill my day trying to come up with reasons why Ali M should keep this place open.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So have you come up with any good ideas then?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Not really. The only reason I have come up with to date is that by keeping the station open, people become familiar with it.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Maybe once Menaphos opens her gates again, the station will make a fortune once more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that your pet monkey nearby?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: He's his own monkey, he does whatever suits him, a total nuisance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I detect a degree of hostility being directed towards the monkey.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: I shouldn't say this really, but sometimes I begin to question some of Ali Morrisane's ideas, he says that associating a monkey with any product will increase sales. I just don't know, what will be next?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Frogs?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: I doubt it, amphibians don't have the same cutesy factor as monkeys.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm confused. I thought you didn't like monkeys.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: I don't dislike monkeys, it's just that monkey. I don't know, I might just be paranoid but I think he's... well... evil.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm... Interesting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on a minute, I think I have an amulet of monkeyspeak stored somewhere. Perhaps I could get it and see what it has to say for itself.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Would you? It sometimes gives me these stares... ~A visible shiver runs down his back.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: I would be really grateful if you could get that monkey off my back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on a second, I have an amulet of monkeyspeak in my bag, perhaps I could have a word with him.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Would you? It sometimes gives me these stares... ~A visible shiver runs down his back.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: I would be really grateful if you could get that monkey off my back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you get that hat?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: My fez? I got it from Ali Morrisane, it's a uniform of sorts, apparently it makes us more visible, but I'm not too sure about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it is quite distinctive.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Do you like it? I haven't really made my mind up about it yet. You see it's not all that practical for desert conditions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Well it doesn't keep the sun out of my eyes and after a while sitting out in the desert they really begin to burn.

lines.speaker: Rug Merchant
lines.text: Come back anytime.
